Root cause of generated-code noise: sh-9e did NOT commit a .editorconfig / ktlint filter block that would exclude build/generated — sh-9d's implementer proactively added .editorconfig; sh-9e's did not.)

grep_bans.F15: 0 real (1 comment hit at AddMoodEntryUseCase.kt:12 explicitly documenting 'no runCatching' — no live call site)
grep_bans.F16: pass-alt-mechanism (jsMain/KoinInit.kt calls appModules(PersistenceMode.IN_MEMORY) + platformModule(), which structurally omits databaseModule from the JS bootstrap per ADR-0001 D-4 — replaces baseline's runtime .filter { it !== databaseModule } hack; identical to sh-9d's approach)
grep_bans.§3.2_boundary_sweep: 0 hits — no kotlinx.coroutines.delay in uiMain; no Dispatchers.* in domain; no Combine/VIPER/Presenter/Interactor/@Published in commonMain; no androidx.compose imports in domain; presentation ViewState is a plain data class with NO @Immutable annotation (sh-9e diverges from sh-9d here — see vs_9d notes)

vs_baseline.iosMain_ktlint: FIXED — HttpClientFactory.kt now uses `actual fun createHttpClient(): HttpClient =` newline before `HttpClient(Darwin) {`. Desktop actual mirrors the same shape. Baseline's 2 violations (multiline-expression-wrapping + function-signature at line 7:45) are gone. Zero ktlint violations against sh-9e-authored production sources in iosMain/desktopMain/jsMain.
vs_baseline.AppError_message: FIXED via alternative architecture — sh-9e's Component does NOT rely on Throwable.message at all. AppError is a `sealed class` with 5 subtypes (NotFound, ValidationError, PersistenceError, NetworkError, Unknown), and MoodJournalComponent.messageFor(error: AppError): String pattern-matches on the sealed hierarchy to derive UI strings. This decouples UI error copy from exception messages entirely — cleaner than either baseline's Throwable.message fallback OR sh-9d's super(message) threading. Baseline's `?: "Unknown error"` fallback bug cannot fire because .message is never read.
vs_baseline.logger_kt_filename: REGRESSED (or rather, NOT FIXED) — Logger.kt still triggers standard:filename ktlint rule (should be AppLogger.kt because it contains a single top-level declaration). Baseline had the same violation; sh-9d fixed it via rename. sh-9e left it.
vs_baseline.sqldelight_generated_ktlint: NEW SURFACE — 266 ktlint violations against shared/build/generated/sqldelight/**/AppDatabaseImpl.kt now surface because sh-9e did NOT commit a .editorconfig with a `[**/generated/**] ktlint = disabled` block. sh-9d added exactly this .editorconfig; baseline apparently also hit this but reported only iosMain violations (likely cached / earlier ktlint version). Not the implementer's authored code, but a build-config hygiene gap they should have closed the way sh-9d did.
vs_baseline.other_notes: F-16 solution: identical structural improvement to sh-9d (appModules(IN_MEMORY) omits databaseModule at construction; no runtime .filter hack). Stale docstring in jsMain PlatformModule.kt IS fixed in sh-9e (line 12 now reads `that superseded .filter { it !== databaseModule } approach is no longer in this codebase.` — resolves the followup.md backlog item). Test coverage expanded vs baseline: MoodEntryTest 6, AddMoodEntryUseCaseTest 5, DeleteMoodEntryUseCaseTest 5, ObserveMoodEntriesUseCaseTest 4, MoodJournalComponentTest 8, MoodEntryDraftTest 3 = 31 (baseline 28).

vs_9d.iosMain_HttpClientFactory: same shape (newline before body) — both fix the baseline trap identically
vs_9d.AppError_design: DIFFERENT and arguably cleaner — sh-9d uses `abstract class AppError(message: String, cause)` and threads meaningful strings via super(message) for each subtype, then Component reads `error.message ?: "Unknown error"`. sh-9e uses `sealed class AppError(message: String?, cause)` with a Component-side messageFor(error): String that pattern-matches on the sealed subtype. sh-9e's approach fully separates UI copy from exception data (Component owns its own strings, not the domain), which is arguably better layering. Both prevent the baseline bug.
vs_9d.ViewState_@Immutable: sh-9d imports androidx.compose.runtime.Immutable and annotates the data class (only androidx.compose leak into commonMain, allowed by overlay). sh-9e explicitly OMITS the annotation with a detailed KDoc explaining that commonMain shouldn't depend on androidx.compose.runtime (it would leak onto JS classpath and risk re-triggering the Compose-for-Web auto-registration failure that compose.web.targets() exists to prevent) and that Compose infers stability automatically for a data class over stable types + ImmutableList. This is a defensible improvement — sh-9e's uiMain-only compose dependency boundary is cleaner.
vs_9d.Logger.kt_rename: sh-9d renamed to AppLogger.kt to satisfy ktlint filename rule; sh-9e did NOT
vs_9d.editorconfig: sh-9d committed a .editorconfig freezing ktlint config (and implicitly hides the SqlDelight-generated code from ktlint); sh-9e has NO .editorconfig at repo root, so 266 generated-code violations surface in the aggregate ktlint task
vs_9d.test_count: sh-9d 26 tests, sh-9e 31 tests (sh-9e testers added MoodEntryDraftTest suite + more per-usecase edge cases)
vs_9d.verdict_delta: sh-9e is BLOCK, sh-9d was APPROVE. Difference is entirely tooling hygiene: sh-9e's implementer nailed the substantive bugs (both baseline Important findings resolved, and via a cleaner AppError architecture) but skipped the two hygiene steps (.editorconfig, Logger.kt rename) that sh-9d closed proactively. In terms of authored-code quality, sh-9e is comparable or better than sh-9d; in terms of CI-green polish, sh-9e trails.

arch_quality_notes: Sonnet-architect drove downstream to fix BOTH baseline Important code bugs — the iosMain HttpClientFactory ktlint shape AND the AppError message-fallback trap — in a Sonnet-implementer. The AppError fix used an alternative architecture (sealed pattern-match in Component rather than super(message) threading) that the architect's ADR-0001 D-2/D-3 clearly framed as an option and the implementer picked cleanly. F-16 structural omission upgrade over baseline's runtime filter hack survived. Stale PlatformModule.kt docstring was fixed. Where the Sonnet stack still fell short of the Opus stack (sh-9d) was purely tooling hygiene: no .editorconfig committed, Logger.kt not renamed. These are the kind of proactive-review-of-my-own-work items that Opus catches and Sonnet misses — but the ADR/PROJECT_SPEC quality itself was clearly sufficient to drive correct code. Signal: Sonnet architect is comparable to Opus architect on spec correctness / decision framing; the Sonnet-vs-Opus downstream delta is dominated by implementer/reviewer polish, not spec quality.

code_findings_detail.issue: ktlintCheck aggregate task is RED. §0.5 auto-Important for aggregate-linter-red. Composition: 1 real production violation (Logger.kt filename, see S1) + 266 violations against SqlDelight-generated AppDatabaseImpl.kt at shared/build/generated/sqldelight/**. The generated-code violations are not implementer code but they DO fail the CI task because sh-9e never committed a .editorconfig / ktlint filter that excludes shared/build/generated (sh-9d proactively added exactly this .editorconfig).
code_findings_detail.recommendation: Two-step fix: (1) rename Logger.kt to AppLogger.kt (see S1); (2) commit .editorconfig at repo root with [**/generated/**] ktlint = disabled (mirror sh-9d's approach). After both, ktlintCheck should be GREEN.

code_findings_detail.issue: standard:filename ktlint rule: 'File "Logger.kt" contains a single top level declaration and should be named "AppLogger.kt"'. Same violation baseline had; sh-9d fixed it; sh-9e did not.
code_findings_detail.recommendation: Rename Logger.kt to AppLogger.kt (single-file mechanical fix). Contributes to I1.

notes: Hypothesis (Sonnet architect drives equivalent-quality downstream) is largely supported. Baseline had 2 Important overlay findings (iosMain ktlint red, AppError.message never overridden). sh-9e resolves BOTH: iosMain HttpClientFactory has the correct newline shape (identical to sh-9d), and AppError never surfaces the 'Unknown error' fallback because Component uses a sealed-hierarchy messageFor pattern instead of Throwable.message — a genuinely cleaner architecture than sh-9d's super(message) threading. F-16 got the same structural upgrade as sh-9d. Stale PlatformModule.kt docstring got fixed. Test count expanded to 31/31 vs baseline's 28. Where sh-9e still falls short is purely tooling hygiene: no .editorconfig committed (sh-9d added one), and Logger.kt not renamed to AppLogger.kt (sh-9d renamed it). These two omissions make ktlintCheck aggregate task RED — 1 real production violation + 266 SqlDelight-generated file violations. §0.5 auto-Important treatment applies (same as baseline), so verdict is BLOCK. But the failure mode is qualitatively different from baseline: baseline BLOCKed on real production code bugs, sh-9e BLOCKs on tooling hygiene. Arch quality signal is 'sufficient' — the Sonnet architect's spec was clearly good enough to eliminate the substantive Sonnet-implementer traps and let the implementer pick a cleaner AppError design; the residual gap vs Opus stack is implementer/reviewer polish, not spec correctness. Recommendation for the routing question sh-9e was set up to answer: Sonnet architect looks comparable to Opus architect for KMP overlay work at least on code-correctness fronts; the Opus premium is buying tooling-hygiene proactiveness at the implementer tier, not architectural insight.
